workspace.renderError: The diagram could not be rendered. Check the syntax and try again.
workspace.fallbackBody: This workspace is wired into the site structure. A dedicated renderer will be added in the next build stage.
workspace.treeHint: Objects and arrays are expanded into a readable diagram tree.

footer.summary: Fast online diagram preview and conversion tools for developer documentation, architecture notes, product specs, and AI workflows.

metadata.title: Diagram Preview Tools - Mermaid, PlantUML, Markdown, Graphviz Online
metadata.description: Preview and convert Mermaid, PlantUML, Markdown, Graphviz, D2, draw.io, mind maps, JSON, YAML and developer diagrams online.

hero.subtitle: Preview Mermaid, PlantUML, Markdown, Graphviz, D2, draw.io, mind maps and developer diagrams online.

why: Use one focused workspace for text diagrams, documentation previews, and file-based diagram formats.
Most rendering runs in the browser, keeping drafts fast while reducing unnecessary round trips.
Every tool has its own URL, metadata, related tools, and export actions so it can grow as a search-friendly utility.

faq.question: What can DiagramPreview render?
faq.answer: The first release focuses on Mermaid, PlantUML, Markdown, Graphviz, JSON, YAML, and structured preview pages for the remaining diagram formats.

faq.question: Do diagrams upload to a server?
faq.answer: Browser-first tools render locally when possible. PlantUML uses a server URL in the first version so the renderer can be replaced later.

faq.question: Can I export diagrams?
faq.answer: Supported renderers expose SVG, PNG, copy, and print-style PDF actions from the workspace toolbar.

mermaid-preview.name: Mermaid Preview
mermaid-preview.shortDescription: Live Mermaid editor with SVG preview and export actions.
mermaid-preview.seoTitle: Mermaid Preview Online - Mermaid Editor and Live SVG Viewer
mermaid-preview.seoDescription: Preview Mermaid diagrams online with a live editor, example templates, SVG export, PNG export, and copy actions.
mermaid-preview.intro: Write Mermaid syntax and preview flowcharts, sequence diagrams, classes, states, Gantt charts, and ER diagrams in a focused browser workspace.
mermaid-preview.emptyPreview: Your Mermaid diagram will appear here.

flowchart.label: Flowchart
flowchart.code:
flowchart TD
  A[Start] --> B{Ready?}
  B -- Yes --> C[Preview diagram]
  B -- No --> D[Edit source]
  D --> B
  C --> E[Export]

sequence.label: Sequence
sequence.code:
sequenceDiagram
  participant User
  participant App
  participant API
  User->>App: Paste diagram
  App->>API: Validate request
  API-->>App: OK
  App-->>User: Render preview

class.label: Class
class.code:
classDiagram
  class Diagram {
    +string source
    +render()
    +exportSvg()
  }
  class MermaidDiagram
  Diagram <|-- MermaidDiagram

state.label: State
state.code:
stateDiagram-v2
  [*] --> Editing
  Editing --> Rendering
  Rendering --> Ready
  Rendering --> Error
  Error --> Editing
  Ready --> Exported

gantt.label: Gantt
gantt.code:
gantt
  title Release plan
  dateFormat  YYYY-MM-DD
  section Build
  Scaffold app :done, a1, 2026-06-01, 2d
  Tool preview :active, a2, after a1, 4d
  SEO pages :a3, after a2, 2d

er.label: ER diagram

howToUse: Paste Mermaid syntax into the editor.
Pick a sample when you need a quick starting point.
Export the rendered SVG or PNG when the preview looks right.

faq.question: Does this Mermaid preview run locally?
faq.answer: Yes. Mermaid rendering is dynamically loaded in the browser for this tool page.

faq.question: Which Mermaid diagram types are supported?
faq.answer: The editor includes samples for flowchart, sequence, class, state, Gantt, and ER diagrams.

seoBody: Mermaid Preview is built for developers and technical writers who need a fast way to check diagram syntax while writing documentation. The workspace keeps source code and visual output side by side so you can spot layout issues before committing changes.

The renderer is loaded on the tool page instead of the home page. That keeps the landing experience quick while still giving the Mermaid editor real SVG output, export actions, and clear syntax errors.
Use this page for README diagrams, architecture notes, product specs, incident reports, and AI-generated diagrams that need human review before publishing.

plantuml-preview.name: PlantUML Preview
plantuml-preview.shortDescription: Preview PlantUML diagrams from text using a replaceable server renderer.
plantuml-preview.seoTitle: PlantUML Preview Online - PlantUML Editor and Viewer
plantuml-preview.seoDescription: Preview PlantUML sequence, class, use case, and activity diagrams online from plain text.
plantuml-preview.intro: Paste PlantUML text and generate a shareable SVG preview through the PlantUML server URL format.
plantuml-preview.emptyPreview: Your PlantUML preview will appear here.

sequence.label: Sequence
sequence.code:
@startuml
actor User
participant App
participant Service
User -> App: Submit diagram
App -> Service: Render
Service --> App: SVG
App --> User: Preview
@enduml

class.label: Class

usecase.label: Use case

activity.label: Activity

howToUse: Paste a complete PlantUML document.
Choose a sample for common diagram styles.
Use the rendered SVG preview in documentation workflows.

faq.question: How does PlantUML rendering work?
faq.answer: The first release encodes the PlantUML source and displays the SVG generated by the PlantUML server.

faq.question: Can the renderer be replaced later?
faq.answer: Yes. The PlantUML URL builder is isolated behind a renderer module.

seoBody: PlantUML Preview helps teams review UML-style diagrams without leaving the browser. It is useful for sequence flows, classes, activities, and use case diagrams that belong beside engineering documents.
The first version uses the standard PlantUML server URL approach. The encoding logic is isolated so a later local or private renderer can replace it without changing the page contract.
Because the editor uses plain text, diagrams can move cleanly between code review, wikis, issue trackers, and exported SVG assets.

markdown-preview.name: Markdown Preview
markdown-preview.shortDescription: Preview Markdown with Mermaid code block rendering.
markdown-preview.seoTitle: Markdown Preview Online - Markdown and Mermaid Diagram Viewer
markdown-preview.seoDescription: Preview Markdown online with support for fenced Mermaid diagrams, HTML copy, and export actions.
markdown-preview.intro: Preview technical Markdown and render Mermaid fenced code blocks directly inside the document output.
markdown-preview.emptyPreview: Your Markdown preview will appear here.

docs.label: Docs
docs.code:
# API Notes

Use this document to describe a workflow.

- Fast preview
- Diagram support
- Export actions

```ts
const ready = true;
```

mermaid.label: Mermaid block
mermaid.code:
# Checkout flow

```mermaid
sequenceDiagram
  participant Buyer
  participant Store
  Buyer->>Store: Place order
  Store-->>Buyer: Confirmation
```

The diagram is rendered inside the Markdown preview.

release.label: Release notes
release.code:
# Release notes

## Added

- Mermaid preview
- PlantUML preview
- Markdown preview

## Fixed

- Cleaner export toolbar

howToUse: Paste Markdown into the editor.
Add Mermaid fenced blocks when a diagram belongs in the document.
Copy HTML or export the rendered result.

faq.question: Can Mermaid be rendered inside Markdown?
faq.answer: Yes. Fenced code blocks marked as mermaid are converted into SVG diagrams.

faq.question: Can I copy the generated HTML?
faq.answer: Yes. Use the copy action from the toolbar after the preview renders.

seoBody: Markdown Preview is designed for technical content where prose and diagrams live together. It helps writers check headings, lists, code blocks, and Mermaid diagrams before publishing to a repository or documentation site.
The preview supports GitHub-flavored Markdown and renders Mermaid fences as SVG, making it practical for README files, architecture decision records, and product specifications.
Use the copy and export actions when you need to move rendered content into another writing or review workflow.

graphviz-preview.name: Graphviz DOT Preview
graphviz-preview.shortDescription: Render DOT graphs into SVG directly in the browser.
graphviz-preview.seoTitle: Graphviz DOT Preview Online - DOT Viewer and SVG Renderer
graphviz-preview.seoDescription: Preview Graphviz DOT diagrams online with browser SVG rendering, examples, and export actions.
graphviz-preview.intro: Write DOT syntax and preview dependency graphs, state graphs, and directed diagrams as SVG.
graphviz-preview.emptyPreview: Your Graphviz diagram will appear here.

digraph.label: Digraph

dependency.label: Dependency graph

state.label: State graph

howToUse: Paste DOT syntax into the editor.
Use samples for directed graphs or dependency maps.
Export the SVG when the graph is ready.

faq.question: Does Graphviz run in the browser?
faq.answer: Yes. This page dynamically loads a WebAssembly-backed Viz renderer.

faq.question: Can I export the graph?
faq.answer: Rendered SVG can be downloaded or converted to PNG from the toolbar.

seoBody: Graphviz DOT Preview gives developers a quick way to turn DOT source into visual graphs. It is especially useful for dependency structures, directed workflows, state machines, and generated graph output.
The renderer loads only on the Graphviz page and returns SVG in the browser. That keeps the home page lightweight while still supporting real DOT rendering when the tool is opened.
Use it when inspecting build graphs, package relationships, state transitions, and architecture sketches that are easier to maintain as text.

d2-preview.name: D2 Preview
d2-preview.shortDescription: Structured D2 workspace prepared for the upcoming renderer.
d2-preview.seoTitle: D2 Preview Online - D2 Diagram Viewer
d2-preview.seoDescription: Preview D2 diagram text online in a developer-focused diagram workspace.
d2-preview.intro: Draft D2 diagram text in the shared DiagramPreview workspace while the dedicated renderer is prepared.
d2-preview.emptyPreview: The D2 preview renderer is coming online.

architecture.label: Architecture
architecture.code:
client -> gateway -> api
api -> database
api -> queue

service.label: Service map
service.code:
web: Web app
api: API service
db: Database
web -> api -> db

sequence.label: Sequence
sequence.code:
user -> app: request
app -> api: fetch
api -> app: response

howToUse: Paste D2 text into the editor.
Use samples to structure architecture notes.
Return after the renderer module is enabled for SVG output.

faq.question: Is D2 rendering enabled?
faq.answer: The route and workspace are ready. The dedicated renderer is planned for the next build stage.

seoBody: D2 Preview is prepared as part of the DiagramPreview tool family so D2 diagrams can share the same editor, metadata, export patterns, and related tool navigation.
The first build wires the page into the site structure and sample system. A dedicated renderer can be added behind the same workspace without changing the public URL.
This page is intended for architecture sketches, service maps, and text-first system diagrams.

drawio-preview.name: Draw.io Viewer
drawio-preview.shortDescription: Inspect draw.io and diagrams.net XML files in a preview workspace.
drawio-preview.seoTitle: Draw.io Viewer Online - diagrams.net XML Preview
drawio-preview.seoDescription: Open draw.io or diagrams.net XML content online and inspect file structure, pages, and objects.
drawio-preview.intro: Paste draw.io XML to inspect diagram file structure before full rendering support is enabled.
drawio-preview.emptyPreview: A draw.io file summary will appear here.
howToUse: Paste .drawio or diagrams.net XML.
Review the file summary.
Use the route as the foundation for future full rendering.

faq.question: Does this render complex draw.io files?
faq.answer: The first version focuses on structure inspection and a clean preview placeholder.

seoBody: Draw.io Viewer is built for users who need to inspect diagrams.net XML files inside a broader diagram tool site.
Complex draw.io rendering requires specialized handling, so the first build focuses on route structure, file input, and summary-oriented preview behavior.
The page is useful as a stable URL for future draw.io rendering and conversion features.

mind-map-preview.name: Mind Map Preview
mind-map-preview.shortDescription: Turn Markdown headings or indented text into a mind map workspace.
mind-map-preview.seoTitle: Mind Map Preview Online - Markdown Mind Map Tool
mind-map-preview.seoDescription: Preview mind maps online from Markdown headings or indented outline text.

mind-map-preview.placeholder: Paste Markdown headings or indented text here...

faq.question: What input formats are planned?
faq.answer: Markdown headings and indented outline text are the first planned formats.

seoBody: Mind Map Preview helps convert structured text into visual planning diagrams. It is aimed at product planning, research synthesis, and AI-assisted brainstorming workflows.
The route is present in the first build so it can share navigation, metadata, and search discovery with the rest of the tool site.
A dedicated mind map renderer can later plug into the same editor and export toolbar.

sequence-diagram-preview.name: Sequence Diagram Preview
sequence-diagram-preview.shortDescription: Preview sequence diagrams from Mermaid sequenceDiagram syntax.
sequence-diagram-preview.seoTitle: Sequence Diagram Preview Online - Text Sequence Diagram Tool
sequence-diagram-preview.seoDescription: Create and preview online sequence diagrams from text using Mermaid sequenceDiagram syntax.
sequence-diagram-preview.intro: Use Mermaid sequenceDiagram syntax to preview API calls, login flows, payments, and service interactions.
sequence-diagram-preview.emptyPreview: Your sequence diagram will appear here.

api.label: API call
api.code:
sequenceDiagram
  participant Client
  participant API
  participant DB
  Client->>API: GET /diagrams
  API->>DB: Query diagrams
  DB-->>API: Rows
  API-->>Client: JSON

login.label: Login flow
login.code:
sequenceDiagram
  participant User
  participant App
  participant Auth
  User->>App: Enter credentials
  App->>Auth: Verify
  Auth-->>App: Token
  App-->>User: Dashboard

payment.label: Payment flow
payment.code:
sequenceDiagram
  participant Buyer
  participant Store
  participant PSP
  Buyer->>Store: Checkout
  Store->>PSP: Create payment
  PSP-->>Store: Confirmed
  Store-->>Buyer: Receipt

howToUse: Paste Mermaid sequenceDiagram syntax.
Start from API, login, or payment samples.
Export the rendered SVG or PNG.

faq.question: Which syntax does this tool use?
faq.answer: It uses Mermaid sequenceDiagram syntax and the Mermaid browser renderer.

seoBody: Sequence Diagram Preview is a focused editor for message flows between users, apps, services, databases, and external systems.
Because it uses Mermaid sequenceDiagram syntax, the source can live naturally in Markdown files, code review comments, and architecture notes.
Use the page for API call flows, login journeys, payment systems, and AI-generated interaction diagrams.

json-to-diagram.name: JSON to Diagram
json-to-diagram.shortDescription: Visualize JSON as a readable object and array tree.
json-to-diagram.seoTitle: JSON to Diagram Online - JSON Tree Visualizer
json-to-diagram.seoDescription: Convert JSON into an online tree diagram with syntax validation and readable object structure.
json-to-diagram.intro: Paste JSON and inspect nested objects and arrays as a structured tree diagram.
json-to-diagram.emptyPreview: Your JSON tree will appear here.
howToUse: Paste valid JSON.
Read the generated tree from root to leaves.
Fix syntax errors shown in the preview pane.

faq.question: Does the JSON parser validate syntax?
faq.answer: Yes. Invalid JSON produces a clear preview error instead of a broken page.

seoBody: JSON to Diagram turns nested JSON into a readable tree so developers can inspect API responses, configuration files, generated data, and AI output more quickly.
The tool keeps the raw JSON beside the visual structure. That makes it easier to find deep keys, array shapes, and unexpected values without losing the original source.
Use it when reviewing API contracts, debugging payloads, or explaining data structures to teammates.

yaml-to-diagram.name: YAML to Diagram
yaml-to-diagram.shortDescription: Visualize YAML documents as readable tree diagrams.
yaml-to-diagram.seoTitle: YAML to Diagram Online - YAML Tree Visualizer
yaml-to-diagram.seoDescription: Convert YAML into an online tree diagram with syntax validation and readable hierarchy.
yaml-to-diagram.intro: Paste YAML and inspect configuration hierarchy as a structured tree diagram.
yaml-to-diagram.emptyPreview: Your YAML tree will appear here.

workflow.label: Workflow
workflow.code:
name: preview
steps:
  - install
  - build
  - verify

kubernetes.label: Kubernetes
kubernetes.code:
apiVersion: v1
kind: Service
metadata:
  name: diagram-preview
spec:
  ports:
    - port: 80

config.label: Config
config.code:
site:
  name: DiagramPreview
  locales:
    - en
    - zh-CN
exports:
  svg: true
  png: true

howToUse: Paste YAML into the editor.
Inspect the generated hierarchy.
Use syntax errors to fix invalid indentation or values.

faq.question: Does YAML share logic with JSON diagrams?
faq.answer: Yes. YAML is parsed first and then visualized through the same tree model.

seoBody: YAML to Diagram helps make configuration files easier to read by turning nested values into a structured tree.
It is useful for CI workflows, Kubernetes manifests, static site config, and documentation examples where indentation matters.
The tool shares tree visualization logic with JSON to Diagram so future improvements can benefit both data formats.

ai-diagram-generator.name: AI Diagram Generator
ai-diagram-generator.shortDescription: Generate Mermaid diagrams from a natural language prompt with AI.
ai-diagram-generator.seoDescription: Generate Mermaid diagrams from a natural language prompt with AI.
ai-diagram-generator.intro: Describe the diagram you want, choose a Mermaid diagram type, and let AI draft editable Mermaid source with live preview.
ai-diagram-generator.emptyPreview: Describe the diagram you want, choose a Mermaid diagram type, and let AI draft editable Mermaid source with live preview.

system.label: System flow
system.prompt: Create a system architecture flow for a web app with browser, API gateway, auth service, billing service, queue, worker, database, and object storage.

api.label: API flow
api.prompt: Create a sequence diagram for a client calling an API, API validating auth, reading database records, and returning JSON.

product.label: Product journey
product.prompt: Create a product onboarding flow with signup, email verification, workspace creation, first diagram preview, export, and feedback.

howToUse: Describe the target diagram in plain language.
Choose a Mermaid diagram type and generate code with AI.
Review the Mermaid output, edit it if needed, then export SVG or PNG.

faq.question: Which AI provider is used?
faq.answer: The server tries Gemini first and falls back to DeepSeek when Gemini is unavailable, rate-limited, or not configured.

faq.question: Are API keys exposed to the browser?
faq.answer: No. API keys are read only on the server through environment variables.

seoBody: Generate Mermaid diagrams from a natural language prompt with AI.
Describe the diagram you want, choose a Mermaid diagram type, and let AI draft editable Mermaid source with live preview.
The server tries Gemini first and falls back to DeepSeek when Gemini is unavailable, rate-limited, or not configured.

petstore.label: Pet API
petstore.code:
openapi: 3.0.3
info:
  title: Pet Store
  version: 1.0.0
paths:
  /pets:
    get:
      summary: List pets
    post:
      summary: Create pet
  /pets/{petId}:
    get:
      summary: Get pet details
    delete:
      summary: Delete pet

auth.code:

info:
  title: Auth Service
  version: 1.0.0
paths:

    get:
      summary: Read session
    delete:
      summary: Revoke session

orders.code:

info:
  title: Orders
  version: 1.0.0
paths:
  /orders:
    post:
      summary: Create order
  /orders/{orderId}/pay:
    post:
      summary: Start payment
  /orders/{orderId}/shipments:
    get:
      summary: List shipments

Review the output, edit it if needed, then export or copy the source.

faq.question: Which AI provider is used?
faq.answer: The server tries Gemini first and falls back to DeepSeek when Gemini is unavailable, rate-limited, or not configured.

faq.question: Are API keys exposed to the browser?
faq.answer: No. API keys are read only on the server through environment variables.

seoBody: Generate Mermaid architecture diagrams from product or system notes with AI.
Describe a system architecture and generate editable Mermaid diagrams for services, queues, databases, clients, and external integrations.
The server tries Gemini first and falls back to DeepSeek when Gemini is unavailable, rate-limited, or not configured.

sequence.label: Sequence
sequence.prompt: Create a PlantUML sequence diagram for a user exporting a diagram as PNG: browser requests export, app renders SVG, conversion service creates PNG, and browser downloads the file.

component.label: Component
component.prompt: Create a PlantUML component diagram for DiagramPreview with web app, renderer modules, AI API route, Gemini provider, DeepSeek provider, and export utilities.

activity.label: Activity
activity.prompt: Create a PlantUML activity diagram for validating pasted diagram code, rendering a preview, showing errors, and exporting if successful.

index.title: Diagram guides for developers
index.description: Practical guides for previewing, converting, exporting, and documenting diagrams online.

mermaid-preview-guide.title: Mermaid Preview Guide
mermaid-preview-guide.description: Learn how to use Mermaid Preview workflows in DiagramPreview with practical examples, export notes, and documentation tips.
mermaid-preview-guide.excerpt: A practical guide to using mermaid preview guide in a developer documentation workflow.

sections.heading: When this workflow helps
paragraphs: Text-first diagrams are easiest to maintain when the source stays close to the documentation. A browser preview step helps catch syntax issues, layout surprises, and export problems before the diagram reaches a README, product spec, or architecture note.
DiagramPreview is organized around that workflow: paste source, preview output, inspect errors, and export the result when the diagram is ready.

sections.heading: How to preview effectively
paragraphs: Start from a small sample, then add structure in short passes. For generated diagrams, review labels, direction, and grouping before polishing visual details.
Use the related tool links to move between formats. Mermaid, PlantUML, Graphviz, Markdown, JSON, YAML, and draw.io each fit a different documentation job.

sections.heading: Export and publish notes
paragraphs: SVG is usually the best export for documentation because it stays sharp at every size. PNG is useful for chat tools, slides, and systems that do not embed SVG safely.
Keep the original text source with the exported image. That makes future edits reviewable and keeps diagrams from becoming one-off screenshots.

about.title: About
about.description: DiagramPreview is an online diagram preview and conversion workspace for developers, product teams, architects, and technical writers.

sections.heading: What DiagramPreview is
paragraphs: DiagramPreview helps people turn text, structured data, and diagram files into visual previews that are easier to inspect, share, and export.
The site is built for Mermaid, PlantUML, Markdown, Graphviz, D2, draw.io, mind maps, JSON, YAML, and future developer diagram formats.

sections.heading: How the product is designed
paragraphs: The first screen focuses on tools, search, and categories instead of a marketing-only landing page. Each tool has its own URL, metadata, related links, FAQ, and export workflow.

help-center.title: Help Center
help-center.description: Find help for using DiagramPreview tools, previews, exports, and common diagram workflows.

sections.heading: Using tools
paragraphs: Open a tool, paste source content, choose an example when useful, and review the preview pane for output or syntax errors.
Supported tools expose copy, SVG, PNG, and print-style PDF actions when the renderer can provide them.

sections.heading: Troubleshooting
paragraphs: If a diagram does not render, reduce the source to a smaller example, check the syntax for the format, and then add sections back gradually.
For PlantUML, the first version uses a PlantUML server URL, so network access to that endpoint may affect previews.

contact.title: Contact
contact.description: Contact DiagramPreview for feedback, support, partnerships, and product questions.

sections.heading: Email
paragraphs: For feedback, write to feedback@diagrampreview.com. For general contact, write to hello@diagrampreview.com.

sections.heading: What to include
paragraphs: Please include the tool URL, the diagram format, a short description of the issue, and whether the problem affects preview, export, localization, or SEO content.

sections.heading: Data handling
paragraphs: DiagramPreview is designed as a browser-first tool site. Where possible, rendering happens in the browser rather than requiring diagram content to be sent to a backend.
Some third-party rendering approaches, such as the first PlantUML server URL implementation, may request a remote preview image.

sections.heading: Contact
paragraphs: For privacy questions, contact hello@diagrampreview.com. This page is a practical product policy page and should be reviewed by legal counsel before commercial launch.

terms-of-use.title: Terms of Use
terms-of-use.description: Terms for using DiagramPreview tools and content.

sections.heading: Use of the service
paragraphs: DiagramPreview provides online tools for previewing, converting, and exporting diagrams. You are responsible for the content you paste, upload, preview, or export.
Do not use the service to process content you are not allowed to use or share.

sections.heading: No warranty
paragraphs: The tools are provided as-is. Renderers, exports, examples, and documentation may change as the product evolves.
This page is a practical starting point and should be reviewed by legal counsel before commercial launch.
